title: koeleria carniolica eriostachya
intro: Koeleria carniolica eriostachya, also known as the Alpine Brome or Koeler's Brome, is a perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of Europe. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects, which can help to soothe respiratory issues and wounds. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving coughs, and promoting the healing of skin conditions. Koeleria carniolica eriostachya is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its effectiveness and safety for human consumption have not been extensively scientifically studied.
